g.text: Formula starts with H and marked (aq)?
YES → skip flowchart; follow red dashed line →
Is the first atom in the
Yes, the first element is a metal
Write the name of the cation
Write the name of the cation
Exception: NH₄⁺ is not a metal
but acts as a polyatomic cation.
(More than one atom, e.g., PO₄³⁻, OH⁻, SO₄²⁻)
Just write the full unchanged name of
No, the first element is not a metal
(Many times it will be C, H, N, O, P, S)
Write the first element using its
Prefixes are used only if there is > 1 atom
(i.e., never use "mono-" on the first element)
Write the second element using the
Prefixes are ALWAYS used on the second element
ACID NAMING RULES — Check Before Using the Flowchart Above
Check Before Starting: Does the chemical formula start with H AND display (aq)?
If YES, bypass the standard flowchart paths completely and apply explicit Acid Rules:
• Binary Acid (No Oxygen present): Hydro + root + -ic acid
Example: HCl(aq) → Hydrochloric acid (Note: Dry HCl(g) is Hydrogen chloride)
• Oxyacid (Contains Oxygen block): Identify polyatomic base suffix change
-ate → -ic acid (e.g., H₂SO₄(aq) containing Sulfate → Sulfuric acid)
-ite → -ous acid (e.g., HNO₂(aq) containing Nitrite → Nitrous acid)
Why NaCl(aq) is not an acid:
Starts with Sodium (Na), not Hydrogen — so it enters the metal branch, not acid rules,
and resolves cleanly to Sodium chloride.
